'''Level 80''' is the 81st level of [[The Backrooms]].
---- ==
> Description
==
L‎ o‎ o‎ k‎ i‎ n‎ g‎ ‎‎ ‎ a‎ t‎ ‎ ‎ t‎ h‎ e‎ ‎ ‎ c‎ l‎ o‎ c‎ k‎ s‎ .‎ .‎ .
{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}[[File:80.png|frameless|254x254px|center]]

I don’t know how long I’ve been here. I genuinely cannot tell if it’s been minutes, hours, or something that no longer has a name. Almost right away, my watch stopped working, but not in the usual way. It seemed like the second hand moved forward and backward a few times before going away completely. When I blinked, it was ticking again as if nothing had happened. Sometimes I hear footsteps. Not behind me. Not ahead of me. Things sound like they're not quite happening as they should, like an echo that forgot what it was meant to echo. {{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}
A note found on the floor in '''Level 80'''. The symbol was printed on the note by [[M.E.G.]]

[[File:W8bet362ksrmr0cw211ap2xrzm.webp|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Clocks Of Torture
'''
'''Level 80''', also known as '''"A Lost Perception Between Time And Reality"''', is what appears to be a seemingly long but never seem to be a seemingly infinite, perplexing level. It appears to be a wooden cabin that houses many clockwork-related structures and objects. This level poses a threat because it manipulates time, memory, and reality itself, similar to levels characterized by hostile environments or dangerous entities. {{Collapse|num=2|initial= {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Clocks.png|text= |width=375px}} |final=
'''...'''
|body=
{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}[[File:80.png|frameless|254x254px|center]]

I keep finding these pages in my bag. I don’t remember putting them there. The handwriting is mine. At least I think it is. [[File:80papers.png|center|375x375px]] I wrote my name at the top of the first page so I wouldn’t forget it. ===It says:=== '''"My name is"''' That’s it. Nothing after. I don’t know if I never finished writing it, or if the rest is gone.
}}
In this level, the very fabric of time is unstable. Rooms warp and move, clocks act strangely, and the continuity between moments breaks. Explorers often experience sudden shifts in how they see things, which can leave them feeling lost, confused, and as if everything is falling apart around them. At this level, knowledge and perception become the main things that get in the way of exiting '''[[Level 80]]'''.
.‎ .‎ .‎ L‎ i‎ e‎ s‎ ‎ ‎ t‎ h‎ e‎ ‎ ‎ t‎ r‎ u‎ t‎ h‎ .
 {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=W8bet362ksrmr0cw211ap2xrzm.webp|text= |width=375px}}
'''Level 80's''' ability to change vision, memory, and time makes it dangerous even in the absence of traditional hostile entities. These effects are typically minor and begin as temporary confusion or moderate disorientation. But as time passes, the distortions worsen, leading explorers to doubt their own memories and experiences. '''Level 80's''' threat is much more subtle because it gradually erodes the mind, as opposed to levels where danger is obvious and instantaneous.

'''Level 80''' has a durable WiFi and fair/average WiFi connection/data. The level disobeys the laws of reality.'''I think it's self-explanatory, [[The Backrooms]] disobeys everything. [[The Backrooms]] has its own universe with different laws anyways.''' The level's temperature stand around '''24-26°C (75–79°F)''' which is comfortably cool. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=AI lolololol.png|text= |width=375px}}

The breaking down of temporal continuity is one of the things that makes '''Level 80''' unique. Events don't always happen in the same order. An explorer might think they've opened a door but haven't yet, or they might remember conversations that haven't happened yet. Cause and effect no longer have the same relationship in '''Level 80''', so results may appear to exist prior to their occurrence.

All of the clocks that can be seen from this level are very valuable and are antiquated, the modernized clocks are usually the ones that can be rarely seen from the level itself, '''since the level itself is very old, or to be specific, it's very prehistoric and has existed since the 17th century.'''
[[File:Almond Water Pool of Level 80.jpg|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> The Relaxing Almond Water River
'''
'''The Relaxing Almond Water River''' is the second zone of '''Level 80'''. It is a river located outside the wooden house, typically the main lobby of the level. '''The Relaxing Almond Water River''' appears to be a regular river filled to the brim with [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Almond Water Pool of Level 80.jpg|text=I apologise for the low quality image here.|width=375px}}
grow, and they often leave people feeling The river always makes a relaxing sound: a low, melodic trickle with echoes that sound like quiet humming. Many wanderers report that they can hear whispers in the Almond Water River, though no recognizable words can ever be identified. The longer someone stays by the river, the louder these words get, and they often make people feel a mix of overwhelming calm and light sadness.

As time goes on, this feeling turns into a heavy, dreamlike calmness that dulls urgency and softens thought, making it harder and harder for wanderers to remember why they were moving in the first place. Basic needs like hunger, thirst, and tiredness are easy to ignore. Instead, a soft desire to just sit and listen takes its place. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Calming River.png|text= |width=375px}}
The air around the river is unusually warm and smooth, with a light scent that reminds {{USERNAME}} of almonds and damp stone. Without realizing it, breathing slows and becomes rhythmic, matching the soft pulse of the glow emanating from the tunnel walls. Some wanderers say they feel like they're being held still by an invisible current that doesn't directly hold them down but tells their mind that they don't need to move.

At random times, the [[Object 1|Almond Water]] starts to give off a soft internal luminescence that makes the whole river glow with a pale blue-white light. At these times, the water's reflections are not accurate; the riverbank's silhouettes seem warped, stretched, or repeated. For a moment, a wanderer may see themself seated where they are not or standing a few meters away, silently staring back. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Screenshot 2026-02-04 204655.png|text= |width=375px}}
Memory has been found to be impacted by prolonged exposure to the river. Names, faces, and individual histories gradually fade away, to be replaced by hazy impressions rather than specific memories. Some people claim to recall locations that don't exist or significant events with no distinguishing details. Often, the only evidence that a wanderer ever had a purpose outside of the river is the written notes they left behind.

Near the water, smooth stone platforms and benches have been known to appear out of the cavern floor from time to time. As if kept clean by an unseen force, their surfaces are always dry, warm, and free of dirt. Many of these places to rest have faint depressions that look like people sitting down, which suggests that many travelers have stopped here before, maybe for a short time or for a long time. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Screenshot_2026-02-04_205739.png|text= |width=375px}}
Even though the level looks calm, no confirmed exits have been found. Some explorers say that going upstream along the river eventually leads to narrower passages and shallow tunnels, but most wanderers die or go insane before they can write about it. People who try to go downstream often walk for hours with no luck and end up back where they started. This suggests that the river may loop in repeating, impossible ways.

Giving up is more dangerous than getting hurt in '''The Relaxing Almond Water River'''. If wanderers stay too long, they often lose the desire to leave and end up sitting by the river and staring into the water with blank looks on their faces. Over an unknown amount of time, these figures slowly fade away, leaving behind only faint marks on the stone and the sound of water still flowing.
[[File:Screenshot_2026-02-04_211207.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Greta's Tavern/Restaurant
'''
'''Greta's Restaurant Tavern''' is tucked away in a forest and looks like it's in the middle of an impossible-to-describe indoor forest. There is a large dining area on this level made entirely of dark, polished wood. It has long communal tables, simple chairs, and thick support beams that look like live tree trunks. Moss grows on the floor and between the wooden boards, and tall plants and lots of leaves fill every empty space, making it look like the restaurant was built around an old forest rather than in it. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Screenshot_2026-02-04_211207.png|text= |width=375px}}
Lights that are soft and amber hang from trees above, held up by thin wires or twine. The light spreads warmly across tables. The glow never flickers, but no power source is visible. There is a strong smell of damp earth, flowers, and freshly baked bread in the air. Even though the land is open and wild, there is no wind, and leaves don't fall unless they are directly touched.

There is never complete silence in the bar. The interior is forested, and there are soft scrapes of chairs being moved, the quiet clink of cutlery against ceramic, and low whispers that fade before individual words can be picked out. These sounds keep going even when there are no other wanderers around, giving the unsettling impression that the bar is always full. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Screenshot 2026-02-04 212322.png|text= |width=375px}}
Plates, cups, and tools are neatly placed on empty tables at random times. The dishes are always warm, like they were just set. Simple meals such as bread, soup, roasted vegetables, and thick stews may appear at any time. This tavern is a place where people usually gather to relax, and especially clocks are mainly seen here, such as '''Grandfather Clocks''', '''Cuckoo Clocks''' and any other wall clocks.

Greta's Tavern was a prehistoric (not quite prehistoric, but it has existed since the 20th century) tavern established by an unknown colonial in the 1950s. There are many types of wines, beers, and spirits that can be seen from this tavern. The food is safe to eat and gives you energy, but eating it more than once seems to make the level's calming effect stronger, making a wanderer want to stay sitting even more.

A sense of familiarity settles over those who enter. Many wanderers report feeling as though they have visited '''Greta’s Restaurant Tavern''' before, despite knowing this to be impossible. This false nostalgia strengthens over time, gradually replacing memories of other levels and places. Faces of loved ones blur, personal histories lose detail, and eventually even the reason for traveling becomes difficult to recall. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Screenshot 2026-02-05 171354.png|text= |width=375px}}
Paths between tables change in small ways that you can't see. A route that once led to an infinite number of plants and trees will now lead to a dining place almost the same as the last one. Attempts to mark trails with scratches, notes, or stacked objects frequently fail, as markings fade or objects are found returned to their original places.

A single, solitary shadowy figure can sometimes be seen moving slowly between the tables. There are different descriptions of the figure, but it is always calm, not in a hurry, and avoids making long eye contact. It never gives a reason for being there. Wanderers who attempt to follow it often lose sight of the figure after only a few steps, even when they can see it clearly.

Giving up is more dangerous than being hostile at '''Greta's Restaurant Tavern'''. Wanderers who stay for a long time stop looking for ways out and instead spend hours or days sitting at tables and eating quietly or staring out at the trees. Over time, these people stop reacting as much and finally become motionless customers who can't be distinguished from the many empty seats around them.
[[File:A clock and a room.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> The Decaying Perception Of Time
'''
'''The Decaying Perception Of Time's''' first room appears to be a tiny, dark room with a clock, two ceiling lights, a door, and a chair that is going to count exactly 5 minutes before the door opens. If you enter through '''The Wooden Shed (Clocks Of Torture)''', it will be impossible to enter this zone. There is a 10% chance that you will enter '''Level 80''' here instead of '''The Wooden Shed (Clocks Of Torture)'''. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=A clock and a room.png|text=[[Level 80/404|FILE NOT FOUND]]|width=375px}}
When the door opens, the hallway will have more clocks, which will tick in 1 minute, and you'll need to escape from a hallway shrinking every second. If the clock reaches 1 minute and you are still in the hallway, the hallway will shrink by 100%, causing you to die to the entities. If you reach the next room, it will be the same, but you'll have 50 seconds, and the hallway will be a little bit shorter than the last one.

You have to run to the end of the hallway before the clocks reach the time limit, and that repeats with each room until you reach five seconds. It is impossible to no-clip in this zone. You'll be sent to [[The Game]] if you try to noclip. It won't work out, so don't even try. Every wanderer who attempted to no-clip has since been pronounced dead, and the [[NO DATA]] may occasionally attack using an unidentified technique, essentially breaking into the room. It is unclear how the [[NO DATA]] occasionally enters the room because the doors are made of metal and cannot be broken. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=An image of Level 2147483047.png|text= |width=375px}}
The doors also can't be opened by a good-minded entity or by an entity that has the capability of doing that, they are made only for humans. This was verified due to many test wanderers have made while being in the level. This proves doors are not that safe at all. A hallway seems to be a room with black-painted walls, ''golden'' clocks in the black walls, and red floor.

You will die if you spend more time in the hallway than is necessary because it will get smaller every second you are there. You will have to do this six times. One minute, fifty seconds, forty seconds, and so forth. Because of the time, each hallway you pass will be shorter. Every hallway's clock will sound when the time has passed and only 5 or 10 seconds remain. Entities are extremely lethal and only occur in the hallway 5–10% of the time. That explains why they don't show up very often. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Screenheytrttrw3rt2845.png|text= |width=375px}}
You will need to complete some obstacles in the same hallway as you approach the final hallway (25–10 seconds). Such as leaping, moving to the left or right, etc. The obstacles are made entirely at random. Following the completion of the final hallway, you will enter a new room. There won't be a way out once the door is locked. The room features a grandfather clock on the floor, a table with fourteen keys, and a powerful red ceiling light at the top. It appears that some scribbles with the words "ARE YOU READY?" and "THE TIME HAS COME" are written on the walls.

These scribbles were probably made by wanderers who have visited '''Level 80'''. There will be a door with an orange button on the right. It will be secured. To unlock it and move on to the next room, you will need the appropriate key. There will be a room with a bridge once you open the door. There will be pipes on either side of the room and a clock on the left wall. To reach the end of the level, you must jump over a lengthy course of obstacles in the room, including boxes, clocks, and tables, because the bridge is only partially finished. But be warned. The clock on the left side of the wall will be ticking. If it reaches 10 minutes, everything in the room will fall over, and you will die. If you manage to get past the obstacles in less than 10 minutes, you will fall asleep and wake up in a random level.
[[File:Screenheytrttrw3rt2845.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> DISCOVERY LOG #1883
'''
This is the discovery log of '''Level 80''', John came to this level, and seems like he passed all the "tests".
{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}[[File:80.png|frameless|254x254px|center]]

''''''
'''Base:''' John, John??
'''Mr. John:''' Hello, I am here.
'''Base:''' Ok, nice to know. What are your surroundings?
''''''
'''Mr. John:''' Ok, ok. I just entered. Anyways, my surroundings are a very dark room with black walls, a white clock, a door and a chair. There are two ceiling lights on the ceiling.
'''Base:''' Alright. Keep investigating the level.
'''Mr. John:''' I'm going to open the door.
''''''
'''Base:''' John? What was that?
'''Mr. John:''' Seems like this hallway is shrinking...
''''''
'''Mr. John:''' I'm don't know where I'm going to anymore... These hallways keep getting shorter and shrinking way more.
'''Base:''' Hmm. Do you think you could go back?
'''Mr. John:''' Let's see. I'm going to look behind me and...
''''''
'''Mr. John:''' [running] IT'S COMING!!!!!
'''Base:''' What? Who's coming? John, are you there???
'''
[[File:OMG ITS THE HOROLOGIUM CONSTELLATION.jpg|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Constellation Horologium
'''
{{Notice|body=This part is a narrator talking to the wanderer.}}
''You find yourself in a very vast of liminality of nowhere, to touch of reality, overcoming perplex trauma every day. Have you gotten over it? Admire this beautiful constellation, as it esteems your whole body.''
To be wise enough, the constellation '''"Horologium"''' is bordered by '''Eridanus; Hydrus; Reticulum; Dorado; Caelum'''; presumably, we're studying the bordered constellations, after all, we can see them in the unaided eye. (Real-life information is mentioned, supposedly to educate the reader). '''Horologium''' is not associated with any ancient mythology. Sir. Lacaille named this constellation to honor Christiaan Huygens, the inventor of the Pendulum clock in 1656. There isn't any mythological association with the constellation; keep that in mind! It may sound like it is due to the naming. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=The Constellation Horologium.jpg|text= |width=375px}}
And keep in mind that the '''Horologium constellation''' does not associate with the main zodiac sign constellations. Honoring Christiaan with its various discoveries and works that had been most important to history, especially its contributions to the astronomy community. Too specific and complex enough; why not hide it for now.... '''Constellation Horologium''' is a constellation that you can see with either a telescope or your naked eye (in the terms of fiction-wise from [[The Backrooms]]. But you can actually see it with the unaided eye in the southern celestial hemisphere. The '''Constellation Horologium''' symbolizes the "Pendulum Clock," which is a clock you can see from '''Level 80'''. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=OMG ITS THE HOROLOGIUM CONSTELLATION.jpg|text= |width=375px}}
It was first discovered by the French astronomer Nicolas Louis de Lacaille, who described the constellation Horologium as "Clock with pendulum and seconds hand". The constellation name was Latinized to Horologium in a catalogue and updated chart published posthumously in 1763. It's unknown how this constellation got into '''Level 80'''; it became its "own property" specifically because you cannot see it from any other level. But there are some missions to map the bodies of these stars and other celestial bodies. As we all know, GLISE 1061, a celestial star body that belonged to the constellation Horologium, has already been planned for mapping. Since there is also a "planet" located in this star, oh, a planet in a level? Wasn't that something a bit off and weird? Well... ok then. But to be specific, the bordering constellations bordering the constellation Horologium can also be seen with the unaided eye or in a simple telescope used for observing. {{Collapse|num=80|initial=> Information about the constellation Horologium. Open for information. Complex enough: Group of stars, considering the diagram.|final=> Information about the constellation Horologium. Open for information. Complex enough: Group of stars, considering the diagram.|body= ==Alpha Horologii== '''Alpha Horologii''' is a solitary orange-hued giant star in the constellation Horologium. It is visible to the naked eye with an apparent visual magnitude of +3.85. It is located 115.0±0.5 light-years from the Sun. ==Iota Horologii== '''Iota Horologii''', Latinized from ι Horologii, is a yellow-hued star approximately 56.5 light-years away in the Horologium constellation. The star is classified as a G0Vp yellow dwarf. It has a mass and radius larger than those of the Sun and is about 50% more luminous. ==Gliese 1061== '''Gliese 1061''' is a red dwarf star located approximately 12 light-years from Earth in the southern constellation of Horologium. Even though it is a relatively nearby star, it has an apparent visual magnitude of about 13, so it can only be seen with at least a moderately-sized telescope. A planetary system lies around this star, and I'm waiting for you, {{USERNAME}}, to be there. Oh, my love, {{USERNAME}}. ==Beta Horologii== '''Beta Horologii''', Latinized from β Horologii, is the third-brightest star in the southern constellation of Horologium. It is faintly visible to the naked eye with an apparent visual magnitude of 4.98. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Cool diagram of the funny clock.png|text= |width=375px}}
==R Horologii== '''R Horologii''' is a red giant star approximately 1,000 light-years away in the southern constellation of Horologium. It is a Mira variable with a period of 404.83 days, ranging from apparent magnitude 4.7 to 14.3—one of the largest ranges in brightness known of stars in the night sky visible to the unaided eye. ==Mu Horologii== '''Mu Horologii''' is a solitary, yellow-white-hued star in the southern constellation of Horologium. It is faintly visible to the naked eye with an apparent visual magnitude of 5.11.}} ----
Something that has been questioning beyond their minds must have been so craving for them. Come and follow us. While staring at this esteemed constellation in the night sky at the level. Look wisely as you esteem this constellation. Something that looks innocent, right? Well, for now, a secret lies among the constellation; I'm waiting for you to be there and meet us.

We've been waiting for eternity, for the outcome. I'm hoping you're designated to it. I'm waiting for you to be here, {{USERNAME}}.
[[File:RomanianMall2.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Entities
'''
Entities in '''Level 80''' appear on every corner in '''The Wooden Shed (Clocks Of Torture)''' and can sometimes appear in the river and the restaurant.

They can be found here:
[[File:W8bet362ksrmr0cw211ap2xrzm.webp|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> ENTITIES - CLOCKS OF TORTURE
'''
[[File:C l o c k.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Pendulum Clocks
'''
'''Pendulum Clocks''' Pendulum clocks take the form of medium sized clocks hanging on the walls of the level. The entity's "skin" appears to be worn and broken, with several chips in the wood and dents in the pendulum hanging inside the clock itself. The clock also has a foggy glass screen, with the pendulum hanging inside. Staring at this pendulum for a prolonged period of time exhibits mental effects on the wanderer similar to that of hypnotizing. After 10 minutes, the wanderer will begin to feel tired. 30 seconds later, the wanderer will have no control over their body, only being able to see through their eyes. After 5 minutes, the effect wears off and the wanderer will go back to normal.

The clock face typically contains 9-15 numbers, each with a different numeral system (Arabic, roman, etc) {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=C l o c k.png|text= |width=375px}}
Notably, the pendulum never actually slows down, despite the lack of a mechanism that would allow it to maintain a constant speed. This is assumed to be an anomalous effect of the pendulum clock.

Strangely, most wanderers report seeing strange hallucinations after staring at the clock, including figures in the corner of their eyes, crates of food that disappear if they get too close, and an exit to the Frontrooms, which sends them nowhere.
[[File:Screenshot 2026-02-05 211922.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Digital Clocks
'''
Digital clocks are very dangerous, but also quite rare. Picking one up will immediately teleport you to a mysterious black void without exception. It could be used as a fast escape from the level, but the mysterious properties of the void may be worse than staying on [[Level 0.1]] or [[Level 630]]. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Screenshot 2026-02-05 211922.png|text= |width=375px}}
No exploration has been done of this void yet, as it is too dangerous and risky. Early attempts to do so resulted in immediate loss of contact from the operatives within a matter of seconds. It is recommended to avoid these digital clocks due to the immense lack of knowledge surrounding this void.
[[File:Level 0.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Analog Clocks
'''
A group of wall-mounted analog clocks that can be seen throughout '''Level 80''' is referred to as '''Analog Clock'''. They initially look like regular clocks in a variety of shapes and sizes, from elaborate brass-faced timepieces to domestic models with plastic frames. But the clocks don't all show the same time. Many have faces twisted into faint spirals, minute hands overlaying hour hands, or digits missing completely. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Level 0.png|text= |width=375px}}
All clocks run continuously even though they don't have visible power sources. Even when the clocks are turned face down or taken off of walls, they still make ticking noises. When multiple '''Analog Clocks''' are found in the same area, their ticking gradually changes into a slow, uneven rhythm.
[[File:Grandfather go brrrr.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Grandfather Clocks
'''
'''Grandfather Clocks''' appear as enormous grandfather clocks, standing between 3 and 5 meters tall. The wooden casing is cracked, warped, and extremely aged, as if it has existed for centuries. The clock face constantly changes. Some explorers see Roman numerals, others see shifting symbols, and some report the face is completely blank except for moving hands. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=Grandfather go brrrr.png|text=So lonely..|width=375px}}
Inside the glass panel is not a normal mechanism. Instead, there is a dark empty space filled with faint glowing fragments that resemble broken memories. Reflections seen in the glass do not always match the wanderer's movements. The ticking sound made by the '''Grandfather Clocks''' never follows consistently. It may speed up, slow down, or stop entirely without warning.
{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}{{Underline|873fcc}}[[File:80.png|frameless|254x254px|center]]
.
If you’re reading this and you still know who you are: Write it down. Write your name. Write your past. Write your favorite color. Write the sound of your laugh. Write the smallest details. Not because paper lasts. '''But because you might not.'''

'''Your memories will be gone soon.'''
[[File:Level -800.jpg|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Colonies & Outposts
'''
Due to the nature of the level, colonies and outposts are impossible to be established.
[[File:L-100 Central Road.png|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Entrances & Exits
'''
[[File:80.jpeg|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Entrances
'''
*Typing "thetest" in one of the computers in [[The Corrupted Instability]] will bring you here. *Touching any clock from any level that is at the time '''21:40''' will lead you here. *Going through a wooden staircase in [[Level 27]] leads here. Any wooden staircase going down to a flooded basement can be used to enter or go back to [[Level 27]]. *Finding a door that has a wall clock stuck in the walls in [[Level 98]], opening it and going inside will lead you here. *In some rare chances, noclipping to a wooden looking floor will lead you here in rare cases.
[[File:Pianp.jpg|class=cover|link=]]
'''
> Exits
'''
*Getting in touch with a modern digital clock will lead you to [[Level 2]], which has a 30 chance to happen, otherwise sending you to [[Level 555]]. *Finding a spider-web that can be rarely seen from '''The Relaxing Almond Water River''' will bring you to [[Level 79]]. *Traversing further more to the forest of the Almond Water river will lead you to [[Level 37]]. *Finding a cool office chair will bring you to [[Level 4]]. *Playing a Beethoven song on a piano will send you to [[Level 81]].
